reasons: Karrie is a clear anti-Hylos answer because Hylos has high HP and wants extended frontline fights.
Karrie punishes that by repeatedly stacking Max HP true damage.

proof.summary: Hylos wants to absorb damage and stay close, while Karrie can convert repeated hits into Max HP true damage.
worksBestWhen: Karrie avoids being slowed inside Ring of Punishment.
failureCases: Hylos reaches Karrie early and stacks slows or attack-speed reduction.
Karrie is forced to fight without frontline protection.

reasons: Lunox can pressure Hylos with magic penetration and burst instead of taking a long front-to-back fight.
Her defensive windows help against Hylos's run-down engage.

proof.summary: Hylos becomes difficult to kill through normal damage, while Lunox can use penetration, sustain, invincibility, and blink options to trade more safely.
worksBestWhen: Lunox avoids standing inside Ring of Punishment for too long.
Lunox saves defensive timing for Hylos's real engage.
failureCases: Hylos forces Lunox's defensive state early.
Hylos re-engages with teammates after Lunox's escape is down.

reasons: Dyrroth can reduce Hylos's Physical Defense and pressure him before he fully controls the fight.
This is strongest in early skirmishes or coordinated burst windows.

proof.summary: Hylos wants to stay in range and slowly win, while Dyrroth can create a defense-reduction burst window.
worksBestWhen: Dyrroth does not overstay inside Ring of Punishment.
failureCases: Hylos survives the burst and turns the fight into a long slow-stacking trade.
Dyrroth dives too deep into Hylos's team.

reasons: Valir can push Hylos away and slow his run-down attempt.
This reduces Hylos's ability to keep Ring of Punishment active on priority targets.

proof.summary: Hylos wants to stay close for repeated aura value, while Valir's pushback and slows help keep him away from carries.
worksBestWhen: Valir saves pushback until Hylos commits.
Valir plays behind teammates instead of letting Hylos walk directly into him.
failureCases: Hylos absorbs Valir's cooldown before the real engage.

reasons: Baxia can reduce HP Regen and shield value while also serving as a durable frontline into Hylos.
This matchup is more about reducing Hylos's sustain plan than instantly killing him.

proof.summary: Hylos benefits from long fights and recovery tools, while Baxia can reduce enemy Shield and HP Regen after marking him.
worksBestWhen: Baxia keeps Hylos marked during objective fights.
Baxia prevents Hylos from freely running at the backline.
failureCases: Hylos ignores Baxia and reaches the enemy marksman or mage.
Baxia cannot keep pressure on Hylos during the important fight window.
